macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Falls for Fifth Straight Week Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 declined 2% last week, marking five consecutive weekly losses for the first time since 2022, while U.S. 10-year Treasury yields rose to 4.43% and Brent crude climbed to $112 per barrel due to Middle East conflict jitters.

items.title: Iran Reopens Strait of Hormuz, Boosting Market Optimism and Easing Oil Prices
items.summary: Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz open during a 10-day Lebanon ceasefire, leading to falling oil prices around $90 and optimistic market reactions as tensions ease.

items.title: Eurozone GDP Nowcast Accelerates to +0.4% in Q1 2026
items.summary: Eurozone GDP growth is nowcast at +0.4% q/q for Q1 2026, up from +0.3% in Q4 2025, driven by stronger industrial momentum and manufacturing PMI improvement.

items.title: Atlanta Fed GDP Now Shows U.S. Q1 Rebound to +0.3%
items.summary: The Atlanta Fed’s GDP Now indicates a modest U.S. GDP rebound to +0.3% q/q in Q1 2026 from +0.1% in Q4 2025, potentially underestimating government spending recovery.

items.title: Fed Chair Nominee Faces Senate Scrutiny on Independence
items.summary: The nominee to succeed Jerome Powell as Fed chair is set for Senate Banking Committee questioning on Tuesday, amid emphasis on the critical importance of Federal Reserve independence.
items.source: RSM Real Economy; Bloomberg (Wells Fargo CEO)

items.title: Wells Fargo CEO Stresses Fed Independence Amid Trump Views
items.summary: Wells Fargo CEO Charlie Scharf highlighted Federal Reserve independence as critically important during a Monday interview, noting President Trump's right to his perspective.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Rises 2.52% to $2.56 Trillion as Bitcoin Finds Support at $76K
items.summary: The total crypto market capitalization increased by 2.52% over the past 24 hours to $2.56 trillion, with Bitcoin trading at $76K after finding support at March highs, signaling positive momentum toward $86K. Top gainers include Basic Attention Token (+7.7%), Immutable (+7.6%), and Toncoin (+5.9%).

items.title: Strategy Purchases $2.54 Billion in Bitcoin, Largest Buy Since November 2024
items.summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy acquired 34,164 BTC for $2.54 billion at an average price of $74,395, bringing its total holdings to 815,061 BTC. The firm financed the purchase mainly through $2.18 billion in preferred shares to minimize dilution.

items.title: $197.75 Million in Crypto Futures Liquidations as BTC Surges Above $76K
items.summary: Forced liquidations reached $197.75 million across crypto futures markets in the past 24 hours, primarily short positions on Bitcoin and Ethereum, triggered by BTC climbing above $76,000.

items.title: Kelp DAO Bridge Exploit Triggers $9-13 Billion DeFi Withdrawals
items.summary: A $292-300 million hack on Kelp DAO's LayerZero bridge led to over $13 billion withdrawn from DeFi protocols and $9 billion panic outflows from Aave, slashing its TVL by over a third to $17.5 billion. Kelp paused the protocol 46 minutes after the attack.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Nears $77K Amid 46-Day Negative Funding Streak
items.summary: Bitcoin steadied near $77,000 as negative funding rates extended to 46 days, the longest streak since the FTX collapse, with negative funding and short liquidations signaling potential short squeezes.

items.title: Major Coins Show Modest Gains: BTC +1.7%, ETH +1.3%, XRP +0.8%, SOL +0.7%
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at $76,265 (+1.7%), Ether at $2,330 (+1.3%), XRP at $1.43 (+0.8%), and Solana at $85.90 (+0.7%) over the past 24 hours, with US spot Bitcoin ETFs seeing nearly $1 billion in weekly inflows.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Plans Release of New AI Chips to Challenge Nvidia
items.summary: Google is preparing to launch specialized AI chips for inference, separating from training chips, amid growing demand and using its own TPUs for models like Gemini.

items.title: Anthropic Withholds Powerful AI Model 'Methus' Over Cybersecurity Risks
items.summary: Anthropic's new generative AI model Methus, trained on 15 trillion parameters, can autonomously detect and exploit unknown vulnerabilities, prompting governments to urge defenses in critical sectors; it was not released publicly.

items.title: Big Tech Layoffs Surge with AI at Center: Amazon, Snap, Meta, Oracle Cut Thousands
items.summary: Over 73,000 AI-related job cuts in Q1 2026 across 95 companies, including Snap's 16% workforce reduction, Amazon's 30,000 jobs, Oracle's 20-30,000, and Meta's planned major layoffs to pivot toward AI automation.

items.title: Apple's Tim Cook Steps Down, John Ternus Takes Over Amid AI Push
items.summary: Tim Cook transitions to executive chairman after leading Apple to $4T valuation; John Ternus assumes CEO role to drive AI integration into hardware and everyday computing.

items.title: Honor Humanoid Robot 'Lightning' Shatters Human Half-Marathon Record
items.summary: At Beijing E-Town Half Marathon, Honor's Lightning robot completed 21km in 50:26, beating the men's world record; over 100 robots participated autonomously, showcasing AI robotics advances.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: War Thunder Returns Rare Premiums and Announces New Event
items.summary: Rare premium vehicles are back in War Thunder, including the AMX A-1A Ghibli for GE, alongside new esports packs like Winter Warrior and Veteran, and Challenger League Major 1 esports news. Server updates and Infantry Mode third test datamines were also highlighted.

items.title: The Division 2 Patch Brings Back Lexington and Turmoil Tomorrow
items.summary: The Division 2 receives a major patch reintroducing the Lexington event along with Turmoil and other updates, as detailed in the latest patch notes.

items.title: Farming Simulator 25 News: Broken F150, T9000, and Witcombe Release Notes
items.summary: Farming Simulator 25 updates include fixes for a broken F150, T9000 details, Witcombe release notes, and more modding news from the community.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Announces John Ternus as Next CEO Succeeding Tim Cook
items.summary: John Ternus, Apple's senior VP of Hardware Engineering, will become CEO on September 1, with Tim Cook transitioning to executive chairman. Johny Srouji will take an expanded role as chief hardware officer.

items.title: US Air Force Cancels RTX's Ground-Control Network for Next-Gen GPS Satellites
items.summary: The US Air Force has canceled RTX's ground-control network project for next-generation GPS satellites due to years of delays and cost overruns.

items.title: Cerebras Systems, Nvidia Chip Rival, Files for IPO
items.summary: AI startup Cerebras Systems, known for its giant wafer-scale chips, has filed for an IPO as competition intensifies in the semiconductor space.
